revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''仙波古墳群'''（せんばこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川越市]]にある[[古墳群]]。 小仙波町・西小仙波町・通町・南通...'
revision.text:
'''仙波古墳群'''（せんばこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川越市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
小仙波町・西小仙波町・通町・南通町・菅原町・富士見町を中心に展開している。なお喜多院を中心に分布するグループを小仙波古墳群、そこから2キロほど離れて分布する愛宕神社古墳と浅間神社古墳を大仙波古墳群と呼んで区別することもある。
== 主な古墳 == *[[三変稲荷神社古墳]] :小仙波古墳群を構成する[[方墳]]。4世紀末の築造。石釧、銅鏡出土。方墳としては県内最古クラスである。
*慈眼堂古墳 :喜多院境内にある。墳頂に慈眼堂、その裏に墓地がつくられ、墳丘は大きく変形している。現状で径36メートルほどの円墳のような外観をしているが、前方後円墳である可能性がある。
*愛宕神社古墳 :「父塚」とも呼ばれている。直径42～45メートル・高さ6メートル。墳頂に愛宕神社の社殿が鎮座している。昭和33年市指定史跡。素環鏡板付轡、大刀出土。7世紀前半の築造。なお前方後円墳である可能性がある。
*浅間神社古墳 :「母塚」とも呼ばれている。径38～35メートル・高さ5.75メートル。墳頂に浅間神社の社殿が鎮座している。昭和33年市指定史跡。出土品は特に伝わっていないが、父塚とほぼ同時期に造られたとみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』pp503-508、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/www/contents/1105419759241/index.html#shiseki 川越市/史跡/旧跡] - 川越市
{{DEFAULTSORT:せんはこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:川越市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''仙波古墳群'''（せんばこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川越市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
[[小仙波町]]・西小仙波町・通町・南通町・菅原町・富士見町を中心に展開している。なお[[喜多院]]を中心に分布するグループを小仙波古墳群、そこから2キロほど離れて分布する愛宕神社古墳と浅間神社古墳を大仙波古墳群と呼んで区別することもある。
== 主な古墳 == [[File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|thumb|right|250px|慈眼堂古墳]] [[ファイル:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|愛宕神社古墳]] [[ファイル:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|浅間神社古墳]]
* [[三変稲荷神社古墳]] *: 小仙波古墳群を構成する[[方墳]]。4世紀末の築造。石釧、[[銅鏡]]出土。方墳としては県内最古クラスである。
* 慈眼堂古墳 *: 喜多院境内にある。墳頂に慈眼堂、その裏に墓地がつくられ、墳丘は大きく変形している。現状で径36メートルほどの[[円墳]]のような外観をしているが、[[前方後円墳]]である可能性がある。
* 愛宕神社古墳 *: 「父塚」とも呼ばれている。直径42〜45メートル・高さ6メートル。墳頂に愛宕神社の社殿が鎮座している。昭和33年市指定史跡。素環鏡板付轡、[[大刀]]出土。[[7世紀]]前半の築造。なお前方後円墳である可能性がある。
* 浅間神社古墳 *: 「母塚」とも呼ばれている。径38〜35メートル・高さ5.75メートル。墳頂に浅間神社の社殿が鎮座している。昭和33年市指定史跡。出土品は特に伝わっていないが、父塚とほぼ同時期に造られたとみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』pp503-508、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[川越市の歴史]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/www/contents/1105419759241/index.html#shiseki 川越市/史跡/旧跡] - 川越市

revision.comment: 参考資料が民間サイトか公的機関のサイトか判然とせず気になりますが、一応出典付きで古墳を追加。
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[三変稲荷神社古墳]]。]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = 仙波古墳群| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|位置図（座標は[[喜多院]]の境内にある慈眼堂古墳）。}}}}
'''仙波古墳群'''（せんばこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川越市]][[小仙波町]]周辺に分布する[[古墳時代]]前期から後期にかけての[[古墳群]]。
==概要== [[新河岸川]]右岸の小仙波町・西小仙波町・通町・南通町・菅原町・富士見町を中心に展開している。[[喜多院]]を中心に分布するグループを'''小仙波古墳群'''、そこから2キロメートルほど離れて分布する愛宕神社古墳と浅間神社古墳を'''大仙波古墳群'''と呼んで区別することもある。
== 主な古墳 == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption =主な古墳の位置 | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=慈眼堂古墳 | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=慈眼堂古墳 | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=日枝神社古墳 | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=日枝神社古墳 | label-size2=13 | label-color2=dark grey | mark-size2=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=三変稲荷神社古墳 | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=三変稲荷神社古墳 | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=氷川神社古墳 | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=氷川神社古墳 | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=愛宕神社古墳 | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=愛宕神社古墳 | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=浅間神社古墳 | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=浅間神社古墳 | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
===小仙波古墳群=== ====慈眼堂古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の[[境内]]にある。墳頂に[[喜多院#文化財#重要文化財（国指定）|慈眼堂]]、その裏に墓地がつくられ、墳丘は大きく変形している。現状で径36メートルほどの[[円墳]]のような外観をしているが、[[前方後円墳]]である可能性がある。
====日枝神社古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の東側に位置する[[日枝神社 (川越市)|日枝神社]]境内に所在する。かつて墳丘上に喜多院の[[多宝塔]]が存在したため「多宝塔古墳」とも呼ばれる。墳形は[[前方後円墳]]であったが、後円部が削平され、前方部のみ残存する。[[6世紀]]中頃の築造{{Sfn|川越市観光案内所|2020}}。
====三変稲荷神社古墳==== {{main|三変稲荷神社古墳}} 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。墳形は[[方墳]]。[[4世紀]]末の築造。[[釧|石釧]]と[[銅鏡]]（[[鼉龍鏡]]）が出土（川越市指定[[有形文化財]]<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=市指定有形文化財 三変稲荷神社古墳出土品|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>）。方墳としては県内最古クラスである。2000年（平成12年）に川越市指定[[史跡]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/smph/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sannpenninarijinnja.html|title=市指定史跡 三変稲荷神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-04-18|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
===大仙波古墳群=== ====氷川神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。仙波氷川神社境内に所在する。直径15メートル×高さ2メートル程の小円墳だが、築造年代は不明である<ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/model-coarse/senbakohun-meguri/|title=川越古墳巡りコース|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=氷川神社古墳|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>。
====愛宕神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「父塚」とも呼ばれている。直径42〜45メートル・高さ6メートル。墳頂に愛宕神社の社殿が鎮座している。1958年（昭和33年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=市指定史跡 愛宕神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。[[轡|素環鏡板付轡]]、[[大刀]]出土。[[7世紀]]前半の築造。なお前方後円墳である可能性がある。
====浅間神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「母塚」とも呼ばれている。径38〜35メートル・高さ5.75メートル。墳頂に浅間神社の社殿が鎮座している。1972年（昭和47年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/senngennjinnjakofun.html|title=市指定史跡 浅間神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。出土品は伝わっていないが、愛宕神社古墳とほぼ同時期に造られたとみられる。
==ギャラリー== <gallery> ファイル:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|慈眼堂古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|愛宕神社古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|浅間神社古墳 </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryūkyō|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

revision.text:
[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Atago Shrine]] to [[Category:Atago Shrines]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square burial mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Tumulus|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>
[[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kawagoe, Saitama]] [[ja:仙波古墳群]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] {{Atago Faith}} [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|方墳" to "|方墳|1=Square Kofun"
revision.text:
[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[Category:new]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>

[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=draft}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|12=draft}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=draft}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=draft}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=draft}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|12=draft}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=draft}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=draft}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=draft}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=draft}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|12=draft}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery>
{{Atago Faith}}
