[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Kofun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=draft}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=draft}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|12=draft}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=draft}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|12=draft}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=draft}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=draft}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|12=draft}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=draft}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=draft}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|12=draft}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=draft}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=draft}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|12=draft}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|12=draft}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:new]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kawagoe, Saitama]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Atago Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]]

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[三変稲荷神社古墳]]。]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = 仙波古墳群| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|位置図（座標は[[喜多院]]の境内にある慈眼堂古墳）。}}}}
'''仙波古墳群'''（せんばこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川越市]][[小仙波町]]周辺に分布する[[古墳時代]]前期から後期にかけての[[古墳群]]。
==概要== [[新河岸川]]右岸の小仙波町・西小仙波町・通町・南通町・菅原町・富士見町を中心に展開している。[[喜多院]]を中心に分布するグループを'''小仙波古墳群'''、そこから2キロメートルほど離れて分布する氷川神社古墳と愛宕神社古墳、浅間神社古墳を'''大仙波古墳群'''と呼んで区別することもある。
== 主な古墳 == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption =主な古墳の位置 | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=慈眼堂古墳 | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=慈眼堂古墳 | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=日枝神社古墳 | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=日枝神社古墳 | label-size2=13 | label-color2=dark grey | mark-size2=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=三変稲荷神社古墳 | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=三変稲荷神社古墳 | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=氷川神社古墳 | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=氷川神社古墳 | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=愛宕神社古墳 | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=愛宕神社古墳 | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=浅間神社古墳 | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=浅間神社古墳 | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
===小仙波古墳群=== ====慈眼堂古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の[[境内]]にある。墳頂に[[喜多院#文化財#重要文化財（国指定）|慈眼堂]]、その裏に墓地がつくられ、墳丘は大きく変形している。現状で径36メートルほどの[[円墳]]のような外観をしているが、[[前方後円墳]]である可能性がある。
====日枝神社古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の東側に位置する[[日枝神社 (川越市)|日枝神社]]境内に所在する。かつて墳丘上に喜多院の[[多宝塔]]が存在したため「多宝塔古墳」とも呼ばれる。墳形は[[前方後円墳]]であったが、後円部が削平され、前方部のみ残存する。[[6世紀]]中頃の築造{{Sfn|川越市観光案内所|2020}}。
====三変稲荷神社古墳==== {{main|三変稲荷神社古墳}} 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。墳形は[[方墳]]。[[4世紀]]末の築造。[[釧|石釧]]と[[銅鏡]]（[[鼉龍鏡]]）が出土（川越市指定[[有形文化財]]<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=市指定有形文化財 三変稲荷神社古墳出土品|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>）。方墳としては県内最古クラスである。2000年（平成12年）に川越市指定[[史跡]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/smph/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sannpenninarijinnja.html|title=市指定史跡 三変稲荷神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-04-18|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
===大仙波古墳群=== ====氷川神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。仙波氷川神社境内に所在する。直径15メートル×高さ2メートル程の小円墳だが、築造年代は不明である<ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/model-coarse/senbakohun-meguri/|title=川越古墳巡りコース|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=氷川神社古墳|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>。
====愛宕神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「父塚」とも呼ばれている。直径42〜45メートル・高さ6メートル。墳頂に愛宕神社の社殿が鎮座している。1958年（昭和33年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=市指定史跡 愛宕神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。[[轡|素環鏡板付轡]]、[[大刀]]出土。[[7世紀]]前半の築造。なお前方後円墳である可能性がある。
====浅間神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「母塚」とも呼ばれている。径38〜35メートル・高さ5.75メートル。墳頂に浅間神社の社殿が鎮座している。1972年（昭和47年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/senngennjinnjakofun.html|title=市指定史跡 浅間神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。出土品は伝わっていないが、愛宕神社古墳とほぼ同時期に造られたとみられる。
==ギャラリー== <gallery> ファイル:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|慈眼堂古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|愛宕神社古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|浅間神社古墳 </gallery>

===小仙波古墳群=== ====慈眼堂古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の[[境内]]にある。墳頂に[[喜多院#文化財#重要文化財（国指定）|慈眼堂]]、その裏に墓地がつくられ、墳丘は大きく変形している。現状で径36メートルほどの[[円墳]]のような外観をしているが、[[前方後円墳]]である可能性がある。
====日枝神社古墳==== 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。[[喜多院]]の東側に位置する[[日枝神社 (川越市)|日枝神社]]境内に所在する。かつて墳丘上に喜多院の[[多宝塔]]が存在したため「多宝塔古墳」とも呼ばれる。墳形は[[前方後円墳]]であったが、後円部が削平され、前方部のみ残存する。[[6世紀]]中頃の築造{{Sfn|川越市観光案内所|2020}}。
====三変稲荷神社古墳==== {{main|三変稲荷神社古墳}} 小仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。墳形は[[方墳]]。[[4世紀]]末の築造。[[釧|石釧]]と[[銅鏡]]（[[鼉龍鏡]]）が出土（川越市指定[[有形文化財]]<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=市指定有形文化財 三変稲荷神社古墳出土品|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>）。方墳としては県内最古クラスである。2000年（平成12年）に川越市指定[[史跡]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/smph/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sannpenninarijinnja.html|title=市指定史跡 三変稲荷神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-04-18|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
===大仙波古墳群=== ====氷川神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。仙波氷川神社境内に所在する。直径15メートル×高さ2メートル程の小円墳だが、築造年代は不明である<ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/model-coarse/senbakohun-meguri/|title=川越古墳巡りコース|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=カワゴエール|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=氷川神社古墳|work=カワゴエール・川越の観光と地域情報Web|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>。
====愛宕神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「父塚」とも呼ばれている。直径42〜45メートル・高さ6メートル。墳頂に愛宕神社の社殿が鎮座している。1958年（昭和33年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=市指定史跡 愛宕神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。[[轡|素環鏡板付轡]]、[[大刀]]出土。[[7世紀]]前半の築造。なお前方後円墳である可能性がある。
====浅間神社古墳==== 大仙波古墳群を構成する古墳。「母塚」とも呼ばれている。径38〜35メートル・高さ5.75メートル。墳頂に浅間神社の社殿が鎮座している。1972年（昭和47年）に川越市指定史跡に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|author=教育委員会 教育総務部 文化財保護課 管理担当|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/senngennjinnjakofun.html|title=市指定史跡 浅間神社古墳|work=埼玉県川越市|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>。出土品は伝わっていないが、愛宕神社古墳とほぼ同時期に造られたとみられる。
==ギャラリー== <gallery> ファイル:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|慈眼堂古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|愛宕神社古墳 ファイル:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|浅間神社古墳 </gallery>

[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built atop it.

[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

[[sn:Senba Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:仙波古墳群]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-in|qq=}}'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, but it may originally have been a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}.
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a {{ill|tahōtō|ja|多宝塔|1=Tahōtō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tahōtō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahōtō|qq=}} once being situated atop it. Originally a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=}} and a {{ill|bronze mirror|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bronze mirror|qq=}} (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City {{ill|tangible cultural property|ja|有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tangible cultural property|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=}} and a {{ill|tachi|ja|大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tachi|qq=}}. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with {{ill|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}} built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

revision.text:
[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-in|qq=}}).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawagoe, Saitama|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawagoe, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the {{ill|Shingashi River|ja|新河岸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingashi River|qq=}}, spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|ja|喜多院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-in|qq=}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}

Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:埼玉県の古墳
revision.text:
[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Saitama prefecture
revision.text:
[[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|仙波古墳群|version=100148253|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
