The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. ==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|qq=|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg

| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|qq=|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. ==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60

| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}

It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].

Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>

A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}

It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].

Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}

Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|仙波古墳群|version=100148253|comment=Imported full ja history}}

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.

[[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Senba Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q17222690}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kawagoe, Saitama]] [[Category:Kawagoe, Saitama]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Senba Kofun Group]][[ja:仙波古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|35.91716666666667|N|139.48947222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17222690]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawagoe Sanpeninarijinjya Tumulus 1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}.]] {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture| label = Senba Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map (coordinates correspond to Jigen-dō Kofun, located within the grounds of [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]).}}}}
'''Senba Kofun Cluster''' (仙波古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the early to late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located around {{ill|Kosennbamachi|ja|小仙波町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosennbamachi|1=Kosenbamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458831}}, [[:en:Kawagoe, Saitama|Kawagoe, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The tumuli are distributed mainly along the right bank of the [[:en:Shingashi River|Shingashi River]], spanning Kosennbamachi, Nishikosennbamachi, Tōmachi, Minamitōmachi, Sugawaramachi, and Fujimimachi. The group surrounding [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]] is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
== Major Tumuli == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|54|44.3|N|139|29|28.9|E}} | zoom = 11 | float = right | width = 250| height =250 | caption = Locations of Major Tumuli | scalemark = 60
| mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|55|01.8|N|139|29|22.1|E}} | label1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-pos1= right | mark-title1=Jigen-dō Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark1=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|55|05.2|N|139|29|26.0|E}} | label2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-pos2= right | mark-title2=Hie Shrine Kofun | label-size1=13 | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=30 | mark2=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|54|54.2|N|139|29|39.76|E}} | label3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-pos3= right | mark-title3=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun | label-size3=13 | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=30 | mark3=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|54|21.0|N|139|29|29.9|E}} | label4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-pos4= right | mark-title4=Hikawa Shrine Kofun | label-size4=13 | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=30 | mark4=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|54|17.3|N|139|29|30.0|E}} | label5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-pos5= right | mark-title5=Atago Shrine Kofun | label-size5=13 | label-color5=dark grey | mark-size5=30 | mark5=Red pog.svg
| mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|54|12.4|N|139|29|20.2|E}} | label6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-pos6= right | mark-title6=Asama Shrine Kofun | label-size6=13 | label-color6=dark grey | mark-size6=30 | mark6=Red pog.svg }}
=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Formerly known as the "Tahōtō Kofun" due to a [[:en:Tahōtō|Tahōtō]] once being situated atop it. Originally a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but the rear circular section was destroyed, leaving only the front portion. Estimated to be built in the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|Kawagoe City Tourism Office|2020}}
==== Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun ==== {{main|{{ill|Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|ja|三変稲荷神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun|qq=}}}} A {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} dated to the late 4th century. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
=== Daisenba Kofun Cluster === ==== Hikawa Shrine Kofun ==== A small {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, approximately 15 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height, located in the grounds of Senba Hikawa Shrine. Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>
==== Atago Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Chichi-zuka" (Father's Mound). A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stirrup fittings|ja|素環鏡板付轡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrup fittings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:大刀|tachi]]. Estimated to be built in the early 7th century.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/atagojinnjakofunn.html|title=Atago Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe City|date=2024-03-28|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>
==== Asama Shrine Kofun ==== Also known as "Haha-zuka" (Mother's Mound). A tumulus measuring 38–35 meters in diameter and 5.75 meters in height, with [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]] built atop it.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Jigendo Kofun, North.jpg|Jigen-dō Kofun File:Kawagoe Atago Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Atago Shrine Kofun File:Kawagoe Asama Zinzya Kohun 1.jpg|Asama Shrine Kofun </gallery> {{Atago Faith}}

=== Kosennba Kofun Cluster === ==== Jigen-dō Kofun ==== Located within [[:en:Kita-in|Kita-in]]'s precincts. The tumulus has been significantly altered due to the presence of {{ill|Jigen-dō|ja|慈眼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-dō|1=Jigen-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539380}}, an Important Cultural Property, and an adjacent cemetery. It currently appears to be a 36-meter-diameter {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, but it may originally have been a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]].
==== Hie Shrine Kofun ==== Located within {{ill|Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|ja|日枝神社 (川越市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509531}}'s precincts. Excavated artifacts include {{ill|stone bracelets|ja|石釧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone bracelets|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a [[:en:Bronze mirror|bronze mirror]] (specifically a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}). Designated a Kawagoe City [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural property]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawagoe.saitama.jp/kurashi/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/sanpenninari.html|title=Sanpen Inari Shrine Kofun Excavated Artifacts|work=Kawagoe City|date=2023-12-11|accessdate=2024-04-25}}</ref>

Its construction date is unknown.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Kawagoe Yell|url=https://www.kawagoe-yell.com/sightseeing/hikawajinja-kohun/|title=Hikawa Shrine Kofun|work=Kawagoe Yell|accessdate=2024-04-26}}</ref>

A tumulus measuring 42–45 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height, topped by [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine (Kawagoe, Saitama)]]'s shrine hall. The group surrounding {{ill|Kita-in|en|Kita-in|WD=Q3080561|lt=Kita-in}} is known as the '''Kosennba Kofun Cluster''', while another group about 2 km away, including the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja|氷川神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawa Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago Shrine Kofun|ja|愛宕神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama Shrine Kofun|ja|浅間神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is distinguished as the '''Daisenba Kofun Cluster'''.
